title: Vatica patula
intro: Vatica patula, also known as the Patua tree, is a species of plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to various health benefits, including wound healing and the management of respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Vatica patula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions and infusions, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Vatica patula is classified as a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It belongs to the genus Vatica, which is a part of the order Malvales. The species is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Malaysia and Indonesia. Vatica patula is also known as the 'kayu balau' and is considered a valuable timber tree. Its classification at the rank of species is currently recognized by botanical authorities.
uses: Vatica patula is a plant species known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. Vatica patula has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant's extracts are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In some parts of its native range, Vatica patula is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal benefits and applications.
